mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate increases discount rates, compressing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts signals collapsing consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a contraction in industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks that force monetary tightening, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.0% signals economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDP growth falls below 1.0% over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while core inflation remains above 3.0%, real rates turn positive and compress equity multiples, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL > 3.0% fails to produce negative 20-day returns with statistical significance over 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate income and consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of monthly observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp manufacturing contraction that triggers immediate risk-off selling and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines greater than 0.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases over a 4-year backtest.
